activities_projects.title: Push and Pull Demonstration
activities_projects.objective: Understand that force can change the state of motion
steps: Place a toy car on a table
Push the toy car gently
Observe the motion of the toy car
Stop the car by applying force with your hand
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that force can start or stop motion

activities_projects.title: Balanced Forces Experiment
steps: Tie strings on opposite sides of the block
Two students pull the strings equally
Observe whether the block moves
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn that balanced forces do not change motion

activities_projects.title: Unbalanced Forces Activity
steps: Pull the toy cart with a string
Increase the pulling force gradually
Observe the change in motion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize that unbalanced forces cause motion

activities_projects.title: Coin and Card Inertia Experiment
steps: Place a card on top of a glass
Place a coin on the card
Flick the card quickly
Observe the coin falling into the glass

activities_projects.title: Dust Removal from Carpet
steps: Hang the carpet outside
Beat it with a stick
Observe dust particles falling

activities_projects.title: Toy Car Momentum Activity
steps: Make a ramp using books
Release the toy car from the ramp
Observe the motion after reaching the floor
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn that moving objects possess momentum

activities_projects.title: Ball Catching Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand effect of force and time
steps: Throw a ball to a student
Catch the ball by moving hands backward
Compare with catching with rigid hands
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand reducing force by increasing time

activities_projects.title: Ball Collision Activity
steps: Roll two balls toward each other
Observe their movement after collision
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand action and reaction forces

activities_projects.title: Walking Demonstration
steps: Walk normally across the floor
Observe how feet push the ground backward
Discuss why the body moves forward
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand Newton’s third law in walking

activities_projects.title: Toy Rocket Balloon Experiment
steps: Pass a thread through a straw
Tie the thread between two supports
Tape an inflated balloon to the straw
Release the balloon and observe motion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students observe Newton’s third law through balloon propulsion
